title: ADCB Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for ADCB Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is ADCB Egypt?
faq.answer: ADCB Egypt is a full-service commercial bank operating in Egypt as a subsidiary of Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank PJSC, one of the largest banking institutions in the United Arab Emirates. The bank was established in 2020 through the acquisition of Union National Bank (UNB) Egypt by ADCB Group. ADCB Egypt serves retail and corporate customers across Egypt with a focus on pioneering digital banking solutions and delivering innovative financial services. The bank operates a nationwide network of branches primarily concentrated in Cairo, Alexandria, and other major cities.

faq.question: When was ADCB Egypt founded?
faq.answer: ADCB Egypt in its current form was established in 2020 when ADCB Group acquired Union National Bank (UNB) Egypt. However, the bank's operational history in Egypt extends back further through its predecessor institution. Union National Bank had been operating in Egypt since 2006, when the UAE-based Union National Bank acquired Alexandria Commercial and Maritime Bank during Egypt's banking sector privatization reforms. Alexandria Commercial and Maritime Bank itself was originally established in 1981. In May 2019, ADCB merged with Union National Bank in the UAE, creating the enlarged ADCB Group. This merger set the stage for UNB Egypt's transformation into ADCB Egypt in 2020, bringing the Egyptian operations under the ADCB brand and integrating them into the group's broader strategy.

faq.question: Where is ADCB Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: ADCB Egypt is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt. The bank's main office is located in Mokattam, Cairo, at 7442 El Gamaa El Hadeetha Street. The parent company, Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank, is headquartered in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, but ADCB Egypt operates as a distinct subsidiary within the Egyptian market.

faq.question: Where does ADCB Egypt operate?
faq.answer: ADCB Egypt operates across multiple regions in Egypt through a network of branches serving retail and corporate customers. The bank has a significant presence in major Egyptian cities and commercial centers. Cairo and Greater Cairo: The bank maintains numerous branches throughout Cairo and its surrounding areas, including locations in Nasr City, Fifth Settlement, New Cairo, Madinaty, Mokattam, Zamalek, Downtown (Talaat Harb), Maadi (multiple locations including Wireless and Corniche El Maadi), Zahraa El Maadi, Shoubra, New Egypt, El Hegaz, and Almaza. Giza: ADCB Egypt serves customers in Giza with branches in El Haram and El Safwa City (6th of October). Alexandria: The bank has a strong presence in Egypt's second-largest city with branches in El Saraya (Sidi Beshr), Glim, Fouad Street, and El Manshia. Other Governorates: ADCB Egypt extends its services to industrial and commercial centers beyond Cairo and Alexandria, including branches in Tenth of Ramadan City, El Obour City (Qalyubia), Mansoura (Dakahlia), and Mahalla Al-Kobra (Gharbia).

faq.question: What services does ADCB Egypt offer?
faq.answer: ADCB Egypt provides a comprehensive suite of banking products and services for both retail and corporate customers. Retail Banking Services: The bank offers current and savings accounts with competitive interest rates, personal loans with flexible terms, credit cards with rewards programs and special offers, mortgage solutions for home ownership, and certificates of deposit and term deposits. Corporate Banking Services: ADCB Egypt provides corporate finance solutions including working capital and long-term financing, trade finance services such as letters of credit, guarantees, and export financing, cash management services to optimize liquidity and cash flow, and supply chain management solutions. Investment and Treasury Services: The bank offers foreign exchange services with competitive rates for cross-border transactions, investment advisory services providing informed strategies for portfolio management, and treasury services for risk management. Digital Banking Services: ADCB Egypt emphasizes digital innovation with its comprehensive mobile banking app available for iPhone, Android, and Huawei devices. The app features biometric login (fingerprint and facial recognition), instant account opening for sub-accounts, fund transfers (own accounts, internal ADCB, external Egyptian banks, and international), credit card management and activation, bill payments and mobile top-ups, loan details and applications, certificate of deposit issuance, and secure mailing services. The bank also provides internet banking platforms for personal and business customers, allowing 24/7 account access and transaction capabilities.

faq.question: What makes ADCB Egypt unique?
faq.answer: ADCB Egypt distinguishes itself through several key characteristics that set it apart in the Egyptian banking market. Digital-First Approach: The bank positions itself as a pioneer in digital banking within Egypt, with a stated mission to be "the customer's top choice by pioneering digital banking and delivering revolutionary solutions." This commitment is evident in its comprehensive mobile banking platform and continuous investment in digital channels. Strong Regional Banking Lineage: ADCB Egypt benefits from being part of ADCB Group, which is the third-largest bank in the UAE and rated A+ by S&P Global Ratings. This connection provides access to international banking expertise, robust capital backing, and best practices in financial services. UAE-Egypt Economic Bridge: The bank plays a significant role in facilitating bilateral trade and investment between the UAE and Egypt, two countries with a bilateral trade volume exceeding USD 6 billion. ADCB Egypt's expertise in trade finance and investment banking, coupled with its extensive network of correspondent banks and financial institutions, enables it to provide comprehensive cross-border solutions. Rapid Growth Performance: Despite being relatively new in its current form, ADCB Egypt has demonstrated strong financial performance, reporting a net profit of EGP 1.1 billion in 2023, marking its second full year of operation. This growth trajectory reflects the bank's focus on customer-centric financial solutions. Comprehensive Service Integration: The bank combines retail, corporate, and investment banking under one roof, offering integrated solutions for diverse customer needs ranging from individual savings accounts to complex corporate finance and trade transactions.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is ADCB Egypt?
faq.answer: ADCB Egypt is a universal commercial bank (مصرف تجاري شامل) operating under the regulatory oversight of the Central Bank of Egypt. As a universal bank, ADCB Egypt offers a full spectrum of financial services encompassing retail banking for individual customers, corporate and commercial banking for businesses of all sizes, investment banking services including mergers and acquisitions advisory, trade finance facilities for international commerce, and treasury and foreign exchange services. This universal banking model allows the institution to serve the complete financial needs of its customer base, from basic deposit accounts to sophisticated corporate financing structures. The bank operates as a subsidiary of a foreign banking group (ADCB Group, UAE) while maintaining its distinct legal and operational presence in the Egyptian market. It is registered with the Central Bank of Egypt and adheres to Egyptian banking regulations and prudential requirements. In the Egyptian banking classification system, ADCB Egypt functions as a commercial bank with universal banking capabilities, distinguishing it from specialized banks that focus on specific sectors or Islamic banks that operate under Shariah principles.
